‘Memo’
Effective Writting 
Communication
Le. (5)


Effictive Writing
Effective Writing:
Writing skill is the ability to write accurate, it is a 
productive skill and involves producing language rather 
than receiving it, writing skill is specific ability which 
help writers put their thought into words in meaningful 
form.


A written Communication
A written Communication:
Means the sending of message, orders or instructions 
in writing through letters, reports, office memos, etc is 
a formal method of communication.


Stages of Writing Process
Stage of writing process:
1.
Prewriting.
2.
Revising editing.
3.
Presentation.


Stages of Writing Process
1.
Prewriting:
Prewriting is the stage of getting-ready-to-write.
In this stage:
the student chooses a topic.
organizes ideas for writing.


Stages of Writing Process
2.
Drafting:
Drafting stage is the time to write down ideas with little 
concern about splling, punctuation and other mechanical 
errror.


Stages of Writing Process
3.
Revision is the third stage, the writer refines ideas in 
his compostions.
Activities in this stage are reading the rough draft in a 
writing group and revising on the the basis of feed back.
Editing stage is the state of putting the piece of writing
into its final form.


Sub-Siklls 
of Writing 
The writing skill includes a number of sub-skills.
Writing Sub-Skill
Mechanical
Word 
Selection
Organization
Syntax


Sub-Siklls 
of Writing 
1.
Mecanics:
Which includes Handwriting spelling and punctuation.
2.
Word Selection:
Which includes Vocabular, Idoms...ect


Sub-Siklls 
of Writing 
3.
Organization:
Which includes Praghraphs, Topics.
4.
Syntax:
Which includes Sentence structure according to different 
grammatical rules.


When we use written communication ?
When is written communication most effective and 
when it is not?
There are number of factors that can help you make that 
choice:
1.
Complexity of the topic:
Using written communication is an excellent choice for 
sharing information that is easily understand by 
independent reader.


When we use written communication ?
This mean that reader can read the communication and 
get the message cliary without additional information
from you or other sources. Meeting notice, Answers to 
quick questions or quick clarifactions are all easy to 
complete with written communication.


When we use written communication ?
2.
Formal Communication:
Written communication is stell the common choice
when the level of formality between two parties.
for example, think about your customers or clients 
chance are that formal communication such a contract 
terms sale, agreement, account information or other legal 
information will be transmitted in written communiation.


When we use written communication ?
3.
Highly Complex topics or lengthy explain
Highly Complex topics or lengthy explain not good choices 
for normal communication.
The amount of explanition required to make the information 
intelligible might be cumbersome leading to misinterpertation
or lack of understanding, in this cases you would have face to 
face meeting or formal training session.


Example of
written communication ‘Memo’
there is example of Memo:
Change Management Memorandum
Date:May,15,2024
From: Jonathan Bennigton
To: All Employees
Subject: Change in Organizational Structure


Example of
written communication ‘Memo’
Pleas be informed of the slight changes in our 
organizational structure, particulary in the credit and 
collection and accounting departments. The management 
has decided to merge the two departments effective 
June3,2024, under the supervision of Kevin Smith.
the managenent expects all employees of the departments 
to cooperate during this transition.
Thank you very much.
communication Le. 4.pdf:
Basic Listening Skills
Le. (4)
Ust. Sahar Hassan


Out comes of the lecture:
• Introduction to Basic Listening Skills.
• We listen for multiple reasons.
• The benefit Improving our Listening.
• Self-Awareness.
• Active Listening.
• the Steps Becoming Active Listening.
Basic Listening 
Skills
Le. (4)


Introduction
A person who is able to listen and understand is capable of 
accurately understanding and interpreting communication.
Listening is the foundation of effective communication. If 
you are incapable of listening effectively, you will often 
misunderstand a message.


Introduction
• Listening effectively is important to developing successful 
relationships.
• Information and ideas are acquired through active listening.
• During listening, it is important that the listener understands
and responds. ( this mean)A will as receiving and understand
what has been said, listening also involves responding to what
has been said.


Introduction
• A skill we can all learn is how to listen effectively. the ability 
to listen, contributes to our feeling of well-being and 
improves our relationships to a great extent.


We listen for multiple reasons
We listen for multiple reasons:
1.
To understand Others.
2.
To gather information.
3.
To learn.
4.
To show empathy.
5.
To build relationships.


The benefit Improving our Listening
• The benefit Improving our Listening:
1.
We can become more productive at work.
2.
We can become more connected in our relationships.
3.
Listening help us to negotiate with others.
4.
Listening help us to avoid misunderstanding.
5.
Listening make life conflict-free.


Improving our Listening
Self-Awareness
1.
Self-Awareness:
An important tool for becoming a good listener is becoming
aware of your own behavior, feeling when listening. 
So, 
If  you use your tense and not relaxed.
or you look down or a way.
if you planning what you will say in return.


A ctive Listening
All of these behaviors make it difficult to be a good listener.
2.
Active Listening:
Active listening means being as attentive and involved in the 
conversation during the times that you are listening as when 
you are speaking, you must learn to be consciously attentive 
to relay.
in order to do this you must pay close attention to the 
speaker.


the Steps Becoming
Active Listening
there are ‘5’ steps to make active listener:
1.
Pay Close Attention:
Look the speaker in the eyes.
Stop any mental chatter.
Don’t start preparing your response while the other person is 
talking.
Notice the speaker’s body language and tone of voice - what 
are the non-verbal messages telling you.


the Steps Becoming
A ctive Listening
2.
Demonstrate Physically that you are listening:
Nod from time to time, when appropriate.
Use appropriate facial expressions.
Monitor your own body language. be sure you remain  relaxed 
rather than and tense.
Use small comments like uh, yes, right..


the Steps Becoming
A ctive Listening
3.
Check for Understanding:
As we learned before our personal experience, perceptions, our 
feeling can influence  the way that hear, it is possible for the 
message to get mistranslated. before responding it’s important 
to check for understanding using these tools:


he Steps Becoming
A ctive Listening
• Use reflecting and paraphrasing, check that you heard 
correctly by saying’
What I hear you saying is......., or 
If I hearing you correctly, 
You’re saying....., or 
I think you are talking about.......


he Steps Becoming
Active Listening
4.
Don’t Interrupt:
There is nothing good that comes from interrupting the speaker. 
you will only be limiting your chance of understanding the 
message because you won’t hear it all -message and the speaker 
will get frustrated.


the Steps Becoming
A ctive Listening
5.
Respond Appropriately:
when you are actively listening, you are showing your respect for 
the speaker, as well as gaining the information that you need to 
form your response. once you have that information and have 
clarified it, it’s time to form your reply. 


the Steps Becoming
A ctive Listening
When expressing your thoughts:
Be honest(truthful).
Be respectful.
Remember that you are modeling excellent behavior for 
others.

Types of 
Communication 
&
Business Report.
Le. (6)


Types of Communication 
Types of Communication Based on the purpose and style:
There are two main categories of communication and they both 
have their own characteristics. and they are:
1. Formal Communication:
Formal communication also known as official communication.
In formal communication,certain rules, conversation and 
principles are followed while communication message.


Types of Communication 
Fromal communication use in official style, usually 
professionl settings for example:
1.
Corporate meeting.
2.
Conferance undergoes.
3.
Business letters, Reports, Orders.


Types of Communication 
in formal communication 
we Avoid:
• Use of slang and foul language.
We Use:
• correct pronunciation
• Authority lines 


Types of Communication 
2.
Informal Communication:
Known as grapevine communication, is using channels 
that are in contrast with formal commnication channels. 
It’s just casual talk.  


Types of Communication 
• It stablished for social affiliation of memebers in as 
organization and face to face discussions. it happens among 
frindes or family.
• In formal communication use of slang words foul language
is not restricted. usually in formal communication is done 
orally. 


Types of Communication 
• in an organization, it helps in finding out staff grievances
as people express more than talking informally.
• Informal communicaton helps in bulding relationships.


Business Report
Business Report:
Reports are a
valuable business
communication
tool
because they help people exchange details and make
decisions.
To making a small business report, you need to be as
comprehensive as possible so if you need to make this
document, then you are at the right place.


Buseness Report
A definition of Business Report:
A formal communication written for a specific purpose 
that includes a description of procedures followed for 
collection and analysis of data. their significance, the 
conclusions drawn from them and the recommendations. 
if required.


Buseness Report
so, 
a business report can be defined as an organised, written 
statement of facts related to a specific business matter. 
It helps the interested persons to get insight into the 
problem and assists in decision-making and problem 
solving.


Why do We Write Report
The main objective of reports is to help a business to 
take accurate and pragmatic(correctly) decisions. we 
compile (need) reports for many reasons:
1.
To carry business information to relevant parties.
2.
To analyes data for interpretation.
3.
To help planning by providing factual information.


Why do We Write Report
5.
To help decision-making by providing necessary 
information.
6.
To help establish effective control systems.
7.
To help reduce and resolve organizational 
disputes(conflict).
8.
To find out the reasons behind a problem.


Types of Report
Types of Report:
Monthly reports:
document progress toward achieving goals/ objectives 
of an organization.
Summary of all organizational activities for the month.
Annual Reports:
Provide shareholders and potential investors with 
information how the company has been performing 
and how it expects to grow in the future.


Types of Report
Technical Reports:
communicate technical information, so the dgree of 
technicality in the report will depend on reader’s knowledge 
and understanding of technical concepts.
Funding Reports:
focus on monitoring the money received to be accounted for 
according to the approved operations outcomes.


Types of Report
Organizational Reports:
that vary from:
Status(state) report.
Progress report.
Daily, Weekly report.
Monthly and Annual reports.


Characteristics of Business Report
Characteristics of Business Report:
As we know, business reports carry information related 
to business activities. 
The nature of business reports differentiates them from 
other reports. the Characteristics of business reports are 
discussed below:


Characteristics of Business Report
1. Specific Issue:
Business reports are written on specific subject. they are 
written to fulfil a certain need.
2. Specific Structure or Layout:
In spreparing reports, certain structure/ layout formats are 
followed.


Characteristics of Business Report
3.
Pre-Specified Audience:
An important characteristic of a business report is that it 
has a specified audience. Usually a report is written for a 
limited number of audience.


Characteristics of Business Report
4.
Written on Past Events:
In most of the cases, reports are written on past events. Most 
business reports carry the reasons for incidents
(problem/issue), the way of recovery(solve), reports are also 
written in past forms/tens.
5.
Maintained Natural:
in drafting reports, strict impartiality must be maintained. 
biased reports may lead to disastrous decisions.


Characteristics of Business Report
6.
Factual Information:
Business reports are always written based on factual 
information. The data collected on spcific events is 
factual.
7.
Joint Effort:
Sometimes business reports are on outcome of joint 
efforts of a group of people-where a committee is 
formed(3-7 people) to furnish a report on certain events .


Stages of Report Writing
Stages of Report Writing:
Threre are Three stages to report writing:
Planning:
1. Define the purpose and scope of the report.
2. Consider the audience.
3. Gather information for the report.
4. Design the structure of the report.


Stages of Report Writing
Writing:
1.
Tips and techniques to write good reports.
2.
Traps to avoid when writing reports.
3.
Write report.
Revising:
1.
Proofread the content.
2.
Do a quality check.


Steps to Write Business Report


Business Report Example for Students
Title Page
• Title of the report
• Prepared for: ( Instructor’s Name)
• Prepared by: ( Your Name)
• Date: .......
Executive Summary:
a brief overview of the report’s purpose., 
finding(results), conclusions, and recommendations. 


Business Report Example for Students
• this section shoud be concise(summarized) and no more 
than one page.
Table of Contents:
• A list of the major sections and subsections of the report 
with page numbers.
Introduction:
• Background: berif background information about the 
company or situation being analyzed.
• Purpose: The purpose of the report and specific issue 


Business Report Example for Students
• it aims to address.
• Scope: The scope of the report, outlining what will and 
will not be covered.
Methodology:
• An explanation of the methodes used to gather data, 
including research tools, surveys, or analytical models.
Findings and analysis

Lecture (3)


Perspectives in Communicaton
Introduction
Perspectives(point of view) in Communication:
We all see the world with our own thinking as known as 
our individual perceptions(understanding).
Our individual perceptions are in actual the filters 
through which we see, understand the world.


Perspectives in Communicaton
Introduction
Also, Individual perceptions 
It is our view through which we handle a situation or 
a topic. all persons has different thinking /opinion.


Perspectives in Communicaton
Introduction
So,
Perspectives in Communicaton:
Perspectives are ideas, views, or fixed ways of 
thinking. these sometimes affect our communication.
For Example,
If you have fixed idea that your teacher or father are 


Perspectives in Communicaton
Introduction
• Strict,even when they are being friendly, you may think 
they are scolding you even though they are polite. In the 
same way others may also have fixed ideas about you, 
which affect whatever you say to them.


Factors Affecting
Perspectives in Communicaton
1.
Visual Perception:
It is the ability to see and to interpret the visual 
information.
Without accurate visual perception person would have in 
adifficulty in learning to read, follow directions, copy from 
white board


Factors Affecting
Perspectives in Communicaton
for example: the famous picture that (you can see as
young girl, and other see it as old woman, it is
reveals(arise)
the
two
perspectives.
Both
of
the
perspectives represented in the young and old woman are
true- they simply two different ways of seeing the same
thing.


Factors Affecting
Perspectives in Communicaton
2.
Language:
Language not only help in communication but it shape 
our perception.various emotion or feeling and 
experience can be influenced by the pattern of the 
language a person speaks.
The same words can have different meaning depending 
how interpret them. 


Factors Affecting
Perspectives in Communicaton
For example: 
what is the meaning of the following sentence?
A woman without her man is nothing.
If you change, what is the meaning:
A woman. without her, man is nothing.


• the words were the same in both case, but the meaning 
has now changed completly. 
• so altough we think our meaning may be clear, we can’t 
always sure the other person will read or hear them in 
that way.
Factors Affecting
Perspectives in Communicaton


Factors Affecting
Perspectives in Communicaton
3.
Past Expererience:
Our past experience greatly affects our way of 
thinking.
It acts as a filter through which we see a particular 
situation.
Our past experience can be positive or negative and 


Factors Affecting
Perspectives in Communicaton
• Imagine(suppose) that you are in a meeting where you 
will be discussing change in your personal policies at 
work, what will you brining to that conversation?
# You might have examples of other company’s policies. 
# you might have example from your own time in the 
company. or
#you might come to the table empty-handed.


Factors Affecting
Perspectives in Communicaton
• What influences you to do any of theses things? your 
past experience,
you would bring outside information because you have 
learned in the past that comparing situations can 
helpful in decision making.  or
you bring examples of your own experience because you 
learned in the past that examples can be powerful.  or


Factors Affecting
Perspectives in Communicaton
you come to the table empty-handed because in the past 
you have felt that your input wasn’t valued or you have 
no past experience in this topic.
So: 
In every situation, your communication is being 
affected by your past experience 


Factors Affecting
Perspectives in Communicaton
4.
Prejudices:(Favouritism)
It is usually unfavorable(unwanted) feeling toward people 
or person because of their gender, age, religion, , language 
or any personal characteristics.
It greatly limit the chances of communication being 
successful or producing the desired result.
We should treat each person as a separate individual and 
must respect his ideas.


Factors Affecting
Perspectives in Communication
5.
Feeling:
feeling is known as state of consciousness such as that 
result from emotions, sentiment or desires.
A person’s current mood affects the way he judges other.
When we feel happy we communicate better but if we 
feel ill, we will not communicate effectively.


Factors Affecting
Perspectives in Communicaton
6.
Environment:
All of us communicate differently in different environment . 
this skill will help us in effective communication.
We speak to our colleagues and friends in different way.
Environment will make the person more comfortable and to 
attain level of formality which is important in particular 
situation.
